overview.tagline: A Small Town with a Big Heart
overview.description: Harrington Park is a small, family-oriented borough in the Northern Valley region of Bergen County. With a population under 5,000 and just over two square miles, Harrington Park offers a quintessential small-town suburban lifestyle. The borough is known for its excellent schools (as part of the Northern Valley Regional district), safe streets, active recreation programs, and a close-knit community that values its quiet residential character.
overview.schoolDistrict: Harrington Park School District (K-8) / Northern Valley Regional High School District (9-12)
highlights: Excellent schools — part of the highly rated Northern Valley Regional High School District
Small-town atmosphere with strong community connections
Quiet, safe residential neighborhoods with well-kept homes
Active recreation programs and community events
Convenient location between the Palisades and Northern Valley

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Harrington Park's housing stock consists primarily of single-family homes built from the 1950s through 1990s on modest lots.
realEstate.recentTrends: Harrington Park's market benefits from the Northern Valley Regional High School District's reputation. Homes sell quickly due to the school system, small-town feel, and family-friendly environment. Renovated homes and new construction on teardown lots command premium prices. Competition from nearby towns like Closter and Northvale keeps demand strong.
neighborhoodNotes: The area near Harrington Park School features classic suburban homes on tree-lined streets
Properties near the Closter border benefit from proximity to Closter's shopping and dining
The southern section near Tappan Road has some of the larger lots in the borough
Homes along Park Avenue are within walking distance of recreational facilities

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by location — check the borough website
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with the DPW

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) collected in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected on alternate weeks
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Yard waste collected seasonally in biodegradable bags

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water) and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges appear on property tax or utility bills
Water quality reports published annually
Report water emergencies to the DPW or Veolia

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Harrington Park Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board or Zoning Board of Adjustment
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Tree removal permits may be required

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Harrington Park is a primarily residential community with minimal municipal parking needs.
details: Street parking available on most residential streets
No overnight parking restrictions except during snow emergencies
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
NJ Transit commuter parking available at nearby Closter or Demarest stations

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth sports, seasonal camps, and community activities.
details: Youth baseball, softball, soccer, basketball, and lacrosse leagues
Summer recreation camp for children
Community events and seasonal celebrations
Borough park facilities with sports fields and playgrounds

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Harrington Park Public Library serves the community with resources and programs.
details: Located at 10 Herring Street
Children's story times, reading programs, and crafts
Adult book clubs and reference services
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Harrington Park enforces noise restrictions to preserve its quiet residential character.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Power equipment use restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise complaints handled by the police department

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards.
keyRules: Lawns must be kept trimmed and maintained
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 24 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of trash, debris, or abandoned vehicles
Trees and hedges must not obstruct sidewalks or sight lines
Fences require permits and must comply with setback and height requirements

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed annually and controlled at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
Barking dog complaints may result in fines

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Harrington Park enforces parking rules to maintain residential safety and order.
keyRules: No parking during snow emergencies on designated streets
No parking within 25 feet of intersections
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
Commercial vehicles restricted from parking on residential streets overnight
No parking on lawns or unpaved surfaces

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Harrington Park has limited rental housing, predominantly single-family homes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before tenant occupancy
Fire safety requirements apply
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Harrington Park Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Tree Removal
other.summary: Harrington Park regulates tree removal to preserve the borough's tree canopy.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees above a specified diameter
Replacement planting may be required
Street trees are borough property and may not be removed by residents
The Shade Tree Committee oversees tree-related matters

schools.publicDistrict: Harrington Park School District (K-8) / Northern Valley Regional High School District (9-12)

publicSchools.name: Harrington Park School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive K-8 school with small class sizes, strong academics, and active parent involvement

publicSchools.name: Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
publicSchools.notes: Harrington Park students attend NVRD; offers rigorous AP program, award-winning arts, and competitive athletics

privateSchools.name: Academy of the Holy Angels (nearby Demarest)
privateSchools.notes: Catholic all-girls high school in neighboring Demarest, popular with Harrington Park families

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey — Mahwah (20 miles)

whyMoveHere: Excellent schools — Harrington Park School (K-8) and Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
Small-town, family-oriented community with a safe and welcoming atmosphere
Quiet residential streets with well-maintained homes and yards
Active recreation programs and community events for families
Convenient location with access to Palisades Parkway and Routes 9W and 303

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes by bus; 30-55 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus service available from nearby Closter and along Route 9W to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Car commute via the Palisades Interstate Parkway to the George Washington Bridge. NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line train available from nearby stations (Closter, Demarest).

costOfLiving.summary: Harrington Park's cost of living is above the national average, consistent with Bergen County's Northern Valley region, with strong schools justifying higher property taxes.

pros: Excellent K-8 school and highly rated Northern Valley Regional High School
Small-town character with engaged neighbors and strong volunteerism
Good recreational facilities and youth sports programs
Strong property values with steady appreciation
cons: Higher property taxes to support two school districts (local + regional)
Limited commercial activity — few shops and restaurants within the borough
No direct train station — requires driving to nearby stations
Small housing inventory means fewer choices on the market
Car-dependent for most shopping and dining errands

restaurants.name: Park Pub
restaurants.description: Local neighborhood pub offering casual American fare, cold beers, and a friendly atmosphere popular with Harrington Park residents.

restaurants.name: Harrington Park Deli
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey deli serving fresh sandwiches, prepared foods, and breakfast items — a community gathering spot.

restaurants.name: Nearby Closter Dining (Closter Plaza)
restaurants.description: The neighboring borough of Closter offers a variety of restaurants including Italian, Japanese, and American cuisine, all within a short drive.

parks.name: Harrington Park Recreation Fields
parks.description: Borough recreation complex with ball fields, soccer fields, basketball courts, and a playground for community use.

parks.name: Memorial Field
parks.description: Community park honoring local veterans, featuring open green space, a walking path, and a memorial area.

parks.name: Pondside Park
parks.description: Small neighborhood park with a picturesque pond, benches, and a nature area for peaceful walks.

events.name: Harrington Park Day
events.description: Annual borough-wide celebration with games, rides, food vendors, and live entertainment for the community.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual tree lighting ceremony with holiday music, hot chocolate, cookies, and a visit from Santa.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade through the borough honoring veterans and fallen service members, followed by a ceremony at Memorial Field.

attractions.name: Harrington Park Public Library
attractions.description: Community library hosting children's programs, book clubs, and local events in a welcoming setting.

attractions.name: Northern Valley Greenway (nearby)
attractions.description: Regional trail system connecting Northern Valley communities, ideal for walking, jogging, and cycling through scenic Bergen County landscapes.

shoppingAreas: Tappan Road — Small commercial area with a deli, pub, and essential services
Nearby Closter — Closter Plaza and Closter Dock Road offer diverse shopping and dining (1 mile)
Nearby Northvale — Additional shops and restaurants on Livingston Street (1 mile)

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Harrington Park Borough Hall, 205 Herring Street, Harrington Park, NJ 07640

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
